message: Jake
Ginny
Tonny
What do you want?
Actually, there's something I need to tell you.
Couldn't that have been dealt with over the phone?
No, it was something we should discuss in person.
Hmph, I don't even want to see your face.
Hey, detective. Don't go any further from here unless you want to be charged with trespassing.
What did you say? What do you mean?
Can't you understand that I don't want to see your face? This is why country folks are such a pain.
Well, I'm sorry about that.
What a nasty guy!

The room is a spacious living room. It's filled with windows from ceiling to floor, offering a grand view of New York.
You're probably using this view to hit on women.
It's more than enough space for one person to live in.
With such an apartment, the rent must be quite significant, but I wonder if that's all covered by debt?
As always, he looks like a man who's the very picture of a conceited person.
He's always wearing a sly grin.
He gives off the impression of being someone you can't let your guard down around.
A gaze that somewhat looks down on others.
You could say he's cool, but to me, he only brings an unpleasant feeling.
I'm too nice for my own good. Letting someone like you into this room.
I apologize for intruding.
If you think that, then why did you come here?
It's unpleasant; I have nothing more to say.
I want you to tell me the truth.
I'm always speaking the truth, you know.
Talking to you feels like a pointless endeavor.
Have you ever had a scalpel used as a prop on stage?
What do you intend to do with that question? I’ve neither been in nor directed a play that used such things.
Then why were you carrying a scalpel?
I can't understand what you're trying to say. I've never carried a scalpel.
It's certain that the scalpel in your hand isn't mine.
Then why did it fall out of your pocket?
I don't know about that fact. I wish you'd stop making up stories.
It's unpleasant; there's nothing more to talk about.
I don't get this guy.
What are you talking about?
The last time
she met was supposedly you, right?
Who knows when the last time I
met her was?
If she met someone after we broke up, wouldn't that someone be the last person she met?
Are you even listening to people?
It's way off to ask me about her. I'm not saying anything bad, but it would be wiser to ask someone else.
You mentioned before that Jennifer might be the perpetrator of the serial murder case.
I don't remember that conversation.
So that was just a joke, huh?
I wouldn't say that's true, would I? Don't just assume you can speak for someone else's feelings.
Haven't you heard at least one of those bad rumors about her?
Well, what do you think?
Jennifer and she did not get along. Jennifer,
apparently holds a deep grudge against her.
I can't say if that's true or not.
I think it's probably true.
That's just my opinion, though.
But you should see for yourself.
You don't know her well enough. She's a scary woman.
You mentioned before that you know the perpetrator of the serial murder case.
I didn't say that, did I? I was just sharing my guess. You should discuss such matters with the police.
I don't know what you're thinking, but I just tried to guess the criminal out of curiosity.
You said that's Jennifer, right?
I forgot whether I said such a thing or not. But, it’s such a recent and rare event that has plunged the United States into fear, so there’s that.
If you’re a detective, why not try to catch the culprit?
I’m interested, but that’s all there is to it. Thankfully, as a man, I can’t be a victim.
Does this look familiar to you?
I’m not a doctor, you know. I wouldn’t recognize a scalpel.
I saw a scalpel.
But I didn’t miss the moment you looked away.
Could you please cut it out?!
No matter how many times I see it, all I can say is that it’s a scalpel.
Does this cufflink look familiar to you?
Ah.
No, I don’t recognize such a thing.
Do you know anything?
No, I just thought it was a surprising design for a second.
You're a persistent guy, aren't you?
Even if I knew something, I don’t think I need to tell you, do I?
What do you expect me to say when you show me something like that?
Is she living somewhere with her wrist cut off, or by now...
Do you mean to say that I did that!?
The one I loved more than anyone else!?
Stop talking nonsense.
Can you please get a grip! I’m telling you to stop.
Just stop it, already.
If you’re going to show me that, bring the person here.
I should have mentioned before, but that’s a ring given by another man.
Regardless of the backstory of how it was given to her,
she was very happy about it and always wore it on her beautiful finger.
Can’t you hear me saying I don't know?
You’re showing it to the wrong person, detective.
At this rate, we won’t get anywhere.
The surprise at seeing the scalpel was anything but ordinary. An undeniably suspicious atmosphere is wafting.
But,
what's going on with him being so nonchalant?
Considering everything,
it's true that he looks suspicious. However,
it's a mystery whether it relates to his disappearance.
Since it's a scalpel, I think it's natural for there to be blood reaction. But, the place it was found was at the Club Kasbah, and he was sitting with.
In that situation,
it can only be considered his possession.
If he is the owner of the scalpel, what was he doing carrying it around? It’s just not normal.
He was last seen,
walking with.
I think it's natural to consider him the culprit,
but there's no reason he should be killed; if he's in that kind of business, he had to know this would happen someday.
The incident's catalyst was,
a murder.
Eva was also attacked by the same perpetrator.
Just because he had the scalpel, it's too early to label him as the serial murder case's culprit, but you can't say he's not either.
He seems to have a motive,
and he has been spotted walking with.
Clearly suspicious.
Anyway,
it might still be too early to conclude him as the culprit. We need to consider other possibilities.
Thanks for the interruption.
Hmph.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
